text: The surgeon begins lateral dissection using a monopolar scissor to remove the abdominal wall with the specimen, aiming for a negative radial margin. The monopolar scissor is used for sharp dissection, while the fenestrated bipolar grasper aids in blunt retraction. After lateral mobilization, the ileal mesentery is scored, and the underwrist vessel sealer efficiently harvests the ileal vessels to the cecum. The right colic pedicle is elevated with the fenestrated bipolar grasper, and a plane is developed between retroperitoneal and peritoneal structures using the monopolar scissor. The ureter is identified and protected. Dissection continues up the right gutter along the white line of Toldt towards the hepatic flexure. Fine dissection with the monopolar scissors allows for skeletonization and high ligation of the right colic pedicle with the vessel sealer. Avascular attachments of the mesocolon and duodenum are identified.

The surgeon separates the omentum from the transverse colon along embryologic planes, opening the lesser sac. Enlarged lymph nodes in the transverse mesocolon are visible with magnification. The monopolar scissors and fenestrated bipolar are used for precise dissection along avascular planes, separating the transverse colon and mesocolon from the duodenum and pancreas, and enabling high ligation of the middle colic pedicle. The transverse colon is further separated from the omentum distally. The greater curvature of the stomach, transverse colon, and small intestine are identified to orient the dissection planes.

The robotic system is repositioned for a second docking, with an eight millimeter port placed in the right upper quadrant and adjustments made to the assistant ports. The patient is rotated right side down, and the patient cart is docked over the left upper quadrant. The surgeon dissects the left colon off the retroperitoneum in a lateral to medial fashion, noting significant tissue edema due to partial obstruction and colitis, which aids in defining the dissection planes. The left colic pedicle is identified and transected using the endorhist vessel sealer.
